brief_notes: Excellent computational neuroscience paper demonstrating the importance of competitive (inhibitory) interactions in whole-brain models across three mammalian species. Strong experimental design comparing cooperative-only vs cooperative+competitive models with clear quantitative outcomes (FC correlation doubling from 0.42 to 0.87 for humans). Multiple dynamical properties assessed (metastability, synergy, hierarchy). Reservoir computing analysis connecting architecture to computation is compelling. Thorough robustness analyses including sign-flipping control, GSR, and different parcellations. Well-cited with appropriate statistical reporting. Cross-species consistency strengthens claims. Minor weakness: some dynamical results described qualitatively rather than with exact numbers.
